---
title: "Investing in Artificial General Intelligence"
date: today
date-format: long
format:
  html:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.html
  pdf:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.pdf
authors:
  - name: Vincent Grégoire
    affil-id: 1
    affiliation:
      - ref: 1
affiliations:
  - id: 1
    name: HEC Montréal
abstract: |
  I model the investment decisions of frontier AI laboratories racing to develop Artificial General Intelligence (AGI).
  Firms choose when to invest, how much compute capacity to build, and how to split it between model training---which builds capabilities and contributes to an endogenous regime switch---and inference serving, which generates current revenue.
  The model combines real options, regime switching, oligopoly competition, endogenous default risk, and diminishing returns calibrated to scaling laws, delivering analytical investment triggers and capacity in a duopoly with default risk.
  A "faith-based survival" mechanism emerges: optimism about AGI, backed by training investment, lowers the default boundary---the hope of AGI literally keeps the firm alive.
  An illustrative calibration to Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and CoreWeave reveals dispersion in implied beliefs about AGI timelines and an asymmetric "AI investment dilemma" in which aggressive overinvestment carries higher downside risk than conservative underinvestment.
keywords:
  - real options
  - AI infrastructure
  - regime switching
  - training-inference allocation
  - strategic investment
  - default risk
  - revealed beliefs
thanks: "I thank Charles Martineau for helpful comments. Vincent Grégoire: HEC Montréal (vincent.gregoire@hec.ca)."
---


{{< include _introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include _model.qmd >}}

{{< include _extensions.qmd >}}

{{< include _calibration.qmd >}}

{{< include _valuation.qmd >}}

{{< include _discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include _conclusion.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

{{< include _appendix.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

## References {.unnumbered}

\begingroup
\raggedright
::: {#refs}
:::
\endgroup
